title: Cut-Dispersion Reconstruction of the Boostless Massive-Scalar Exchange Trispectrum
one_sentence_idea: Use the 2112.03448 cutting rule as discontinuity data, then reconstruct the full scalar four-point exchange correlator by momentum-space dispersion, fixing the remaining contact ambiguity with the boostless contact-trispectrum basis.
motivation: The seed paper arXiv:2112.03448 gives an efficient way to isolate local versus nonlocal cosmological-collider signals from massive-particle evolution, but mainly as signal extraction. A natural next step is to turn those cuts into a full-shape calculation of the scalar trispectrum without doing the original nested in-in time integrals. This sits between known contact reconstruction for scalar trispectra and newer discontinuity/dispersion methods, and it targets the open exchange-trispectrum problem rather than another contact-term classification.
calculation_target: Compute the tree-level four-point function <zeta zeta zeta zeta> from one exchanged massive scalar sigma in a boost-breaking EFT of inflation, starting with the minimal cubic pair dot-zeta^2 sigma at both vertices, then adding the first spatial-derivative vertex (partial_i zeta)^2 sigma as the first nontrivial boostless extension. The first explicit deliverable is the collapsed-channel nonanalytic part plus a dispersion-reconstructed full s-channel shape, up to a finite contact polynomial fixed by the known contact basis and soft/flat-space limits.
method_sketch: Start with conformal time mode functions for massless external scalars and a principal-series massive sigma. Use the 2112.03448 cut rule to write the s-channel discontinuity/nonlocal signal as a product of two cubic sub-correlators. Treat this discontinuity as input to a one-variable dispersion relation in the internal energy or collapsed momentum variable. Reconstruct the exchange correlator, then add the most general boostless local contact trispectrum at the same derivative order and fix its coefficients by requiring correct total-energy singularity, absence of spurious folded poles, permutation symmetry across s/t/u channels, and agreement with the heavy-mass local EFT expansion.
first_checks: Reproduce the known collapsed-limit oscillation ~ (k_L/k_S)^{3/2 +/- i mu} and its local/nonlocal split for the minimal dot-zeta^2 sigma exchange.
Check that the heavy-mass expansion of the reconstructed result matches the local contact trispectrum generated by integrating out sigma.
Verify that the discontinuity of the final expression returns the 2112.03448 cut input and that crossing over s/t/u channels introduces no spurious singularities.
Benchmark the conformally-coupled-scalar limit against the scalar-exchange seed solution used in the cosmological bootstrap literature before moving to massless zeta.
literature_checks: ARC MCP paper tools were attempted first for arXiv:2112.03448 and a batch of related IDs, but the tool returned 'user cancelled MCP tool call'; I therefore used the supplied ARC domain markdown plus primary arXiv pages.
arXiv:2112.03448 establishes the local/nonlocal signal classification and analytic cutting rule for cosmological collider signals.
arXiv:2106.15468 derives the general scalar contact trispectrum for manifestly local contact interactions, including boostless amplitude input and contact reconstruction.
arXiv:2602.03841 and arXiv:2602.05546 support the discontinuity-to-dispersion route for cosmological correlators, but the latter explicitly notes contact-diagram ambiguities.

arXiv:2512.07941 already analyzes the local/nonlocal split for single massive-scalar exchange through open EFT/Keldysh language, so the proposed novelty should be framed as a full-shape dispersion reconstruction for boostless scalar trispectra, not merely as rediscovering the split.
key_papers: arXiv:2112.03448, Cutting Rule for Cosmological Collider Signals: A Bulk Evolution Perspective, https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.03448
arXiv:1811.00024, The Cosmological Bootstrap: Inflationary Correlators from Symmetries and Singularities, https://arxiv.org/abs/1811.00024
arXiv:2106.15468, From Amplitudes to Contact Cosmological Correlators, https://arxiv.org/abs/2106.15468
arXiv:2602.03841, Cosmological Correlator Discontinuities from Scattering Amplitudes, https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.03841
arXiv:2602.05546, Reconstructing cosmological correlators via dispersion: from cutting to dressing rules, https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.05546
arXiv:2512.07941, Open Effective Field Theory and the Physics of Cosmological Collider Signals, https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.07941
novelty_uncertainty: Moderate. The ingredients exist separately: scalar exchange seeds, contact reconstruction, cutting rules, and dispersion reconstruction. The uncertain part is whether a recent 2026 paper has already carried out exactly this boostless massless-scalar exchange trispectrum case. To reduce risk, the project should emphasize a concrete derivative-order classification, explicit contact-ambiguity fixing, and a usable analytic/separable full-shape expression rather than only the nonanalytic collider signal.
response_to_reviewer: If the reviewer says the nonlocal collapsed signal is already known, agree and narrow the claim: the paper is not claiming a new squeezed-limit signal. The new calculational object is the full boostless scalar-exchange trispectrum reconstructed from cuts, with the local contact ambiguity fixed systematically and checked against the heavy-mass EFT limit.
